heading: Providing transportation and service
label: 14101 Providing transportation and service
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11101 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided in , see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1996Pub. L. 104–88section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88section 1301 of this title
amendments: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by .section 11101 of this titlePub. L. 104–88, § 102(a)

children.heading: .—On Reasonable Request
children.label: a .—On Reasonable Request
children.text: A carrier providing transportation or service subject to jurisdiction under chapter 135 shall provide the transportation or service on reasonable request. In addition, a motor carrier shall provide safe and adequate service, equipment, and facilities.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A carrier providing transportation or service subject to jurisdiction under chapter 135 may enter into a contract with a shipper, other than for the movement of household goods described in section 13102(10)(A), to provide specified services under specified rates and conditions. If the shipper and carrier, in writing, expressly waive any or all rights and remedies under this part for the transportation covered by the contract, the transportation provided under the contract shall not be subject to the waived rights and remedies and may not be subsequently challenged on the ground that it violates the waived rights and remedies. The parties may not waive the provisions governing registration, insurance, or safety fitness.

children.heading: .—Remedy for breach of contract
children.label: 2 .—Remedy for breach of contract
children.text: The exclusive remedy for any alleged breach of a contract entered into under this subsection shall be an action in an appropriate State court or United States district court, unless the parties otherwise agree.
